message:
fix(core): add column-default evaluation re-entrancy guard and harden page cache eviction

Column default re-entrancy (connection.rs):
- Add column_default_eval_depth counter with ColumnDefaultEvalGuard RAII
  wrapper to detect and prevent recursive column-default evaluation
- When runtime-input construction triggers a nested SELECT to evaluate a
  column default expression, the same default map rebuild could be entered
  recursively — the new guard detects depth > 1 and falls back to a NULL
  default rather than entering an infinite loop or stack overflow
- Add resolve_column_default_with_guard() which increments the depth
  counter, checks for re-entrancy, and delegates to the existing default
  resolution logic only at depth 1
- Wire the guard through the INSERT runtime-input construction path and
  the CREATE TABLE default-expression validation path
- Add comprehensive tests: simple non-recursive defaults still work,
  recursive default evaluation terminates with NULL fallback, depth
  counter properly resets after guard drop

Page cache eviction hardening (page_cache.rs):
- Refactor the LRU eviction loop to properly handle dirty page writeback
  failures: when a dirty page cannot be flushed during eviction, skip it
  and try the next candidate rather than aborting the entire eviction pass
- Add eviction attempt counter to prevent infinite loops when all candidate
  pages are dirty and unflushed — bail out after visiting every candidate
  once
- Consolidate the eviction path to avoid redundant dirty-page checks by
  separating the "find candidate" and "evict candidate" phases
- Add metrics tracking for eviction passes: cache hits, misses, evictions,
  and writeback failures surfaced via tracing spans

Re-export (lib.rs):
- Re-export FrankenStorage from the top-level crate for downstream
  consumers (coding_agent_session_search) that reference it via the
  frankensqlite crate path rather than the internal fsqlite-core path

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
